title: Culturing THP-1 Cells
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"48pd2","text":"THP-1 cells are a human monocyte suspension cell line from peripheral blood of a 1 year old infant who had acute mnocytic leukemia. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: The base medium for this cell line is RMPI-1640

Reguired supplements: 1 % volume, L-glutamine 10 % volume, Fetal Bovine Serum Most catalog numbers of RMPI-1640 contain L-glutamine, however, some do not. Ensure that it is in the media before using it for culturing.

Optional Supplements: 1 % volume, PenStrep 0.05 millimolar (mM), 2-mercaptoethanol PenStrep is not required for THP-1 culturing, however, if you are having issues with bacterial contamination, it can be used at 1X. 2-mercaptoethanol is stated as a required component for complete RPMI-1640 medium, however, in our laboratory it is not standard practice to add it.
Always store cells in liquid nitrogen. This is for both the original tube of cells from ATCC and any passages afterwards.
Place the media bottle in the 37 °C, water bath at least 00:30:00, prior to using
Thaw cells at 25 °C, (room temperature) for 00:10:00 or 37 °C, in a water bath for 00:02:00
As soon as the cells are thawed, transfer the cells to a 15 mL, conical tube and add 10 mL, of complete media Cells are stored with 5% DMSO, which can lyse cells if they are left for too long.
Pellet cells for 00:03:00, at 500g
Discard supernatant
Resuspend cells by pipetting up and down 5X in 5 mL, complete media
Sanitize all items going into the Biological Safety Cabinet with 70% ethanol
Transfer cells + media to a T-25 flask
Incubate cells at 37 °C, and 5% CO2 and 80% humidity
THP-1 cells replicate after ~26 hours. In practice, it takes 2 days for a true doubling.
Once cells have doubled OR when media has begun to change colour, it is time to add media, split cells into new flasks, or to spin down to remove all media
If concerned about cell concentration, perform a cell count
Double the total media volume with new complete media
If concerned about cell concentration, perform a cell count
Carefully mix the new media in by rocking the flask back and forth
Once the volume limit is reached for the T-25 flask it is time to move to a T-175 flask
Remove all cell + media from the T-25 flask and transfer them to the T-175 flask This has to be done with a 10 mL, pipette because the larger volume pipettes do not fit in the T-25.
Double the total media volume with new complete media
If cells are appearing unhappy, it may be beneficial to remove all of the current media and dead cells and replenish with new complete media
Transfer all cell + media to 50mL conical tubes
Pellet cells for 00:03:00, at 500g
Discard supernatant

Resuspend cells by pipetting up and down 5X in new complete media. Unless cells were at a drastically low cell concentration, resuspend cells in the same volume of media they were in before pelleting.
Transfer cells to a new T-flask
Place the flask back in the incubator
Place all flasks in the incubator
Place flask into the incubator
creator: Teesha C Luehr
